message:
fix(loader): warn when multiple preferred JSONL files exist in .beads/

find_jsonl_path() previously silently chose the first match from the
preference list (beads.jsonl > issues.jsonl > beads.base.jsonl). When
multiple existed, operators had no indication which file was being used.
Now emits a tracing::warn with the chosen and ignored file names.

Addresses W4 from the bd-2bpl.4.3 workspace/hook/repo-path audit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4","title":"Audit and close export/pages/workspace integration parity gaps","description":"Background:
Legacy `bv` is more than robot JSON and a TUI. It also includes export hooks, workspace-aware behavior, pages export and preview flows, and operator-facing deployment workflows that can quietly diverge when path semantics or bundle contracts differ.

Goal:
Rebuild confidence in the integration surfaces by auditing and then closing gaps in static export artifacts, preview/watch behavior, pages wizard flows, workspace aggregation, and hook execution semantics.

Acceptance:
- Export artifacts and runtime bundle semantics are explicitly checked against legacy expectations.
- Preview/watch/pages workflows are validated for both happy-path and failure-path behavior.
- Workspace and repo-path semantics are proven consistent across integration surfaces.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.154964889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.149035060Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","pages","parity","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.154964889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":130,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Integration track guidance:
Treat path-resolution semantics as a recurring root-cause category. Recent bugs in hooks, feedback storage, preview serving, and wizard repair flow all came from seemingly minor cwd/repo/workspace assumptions. Audit those assumptions first, then fix bundle/runtime behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","title":"Re-audit static export bundle semantics and viewer-runtime contracts","description":"Purpose:
Audit the exported bundle as a product artifact: asset inventory, bootstrap payloads, offline/static-host assumptions, CSP/cache behavior, SQLite or JSON sidecars, and viewer startup expectations.

Why this matters:
A pages export that exists but does not behave like the legacy artifact is not real parity.

Done means:
We have a concrete list of matching and missing bundle semantics, plus any required fix tasks.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.937867751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T21:58:55.942860334Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T21:58:55.698935661Z","close_reason":"Completed export/runtime contract audit slice: landed shared triage payload parity for project_health and quick_ref counters, updated contract test to reflect must-have-now status, and left history timeline as the documented deferred gap.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bundle","export","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:32.256517849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.937867751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Using bv robot triage to pick the next feasible slice. Current top pick is bd-2bpl.3.2, but src/tui.rs is already actively dirty, so I am taking bd-2bpl.4.1 as the highest-impact export/runtime bead I can move without collision. I am auditing static bundle contracts in src/export_pages.rs, tests/export_pages.rs, and viewer assets, and I will land concrete export-contract fixes instead of just notes.","created_at":"2026-03-15T21:51:52Z"},{"id":160,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Audit/update complete: triage payload now exports project_health plus legacy quick_ref counters (open_count/actionable_count/blocked_count/in_progress_count), so both --robot-triage and pages export share the richer contract the viewer/schema already expected. I updated the export contract matrix to mark those two items must-have-now and verified with rch via cargo test --lib triage_, cargo test --test export_pages export_pages_contract_makes_current_parity_status_explicit -- --exact, cargo check --all-targets, and cargo clippy --all-targets. Remaining explicit deferred gap in this lane is the richer history timeline schema, which is still documented in tests/export_pages.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-15T21:58:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","title":"Re-audit preview/watch/pages wizard workflows including failure-path operator experience","description":"Purpose:
Audit the end-to-end pages workflow: export, preview, live reload, watch loops, deploy-target configuration, saved config repair, and wizard transcripts or operator guidance. Failure paths matter as much as happy paths.

Acceptance:
The bead identifies where the workflow is equivalent, where it is weaker, and which failures still confuse or strand the operator.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.282086233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T03:16:17.365538662Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T03:16:17.364921017Z","close_reason":"Audit complete. 15 gaps documented (5 critical, 5 significant, 5 minor). Key findings: no pre-flight validation or atomic export (partial bundles on failure), no preview server read timeout, watch mode silently skips inaccessible files, wizard export failure non-resumable. Prior agents fixed 4 bugs during audit. Full findings in comment for bd-2bpl.4.4.2.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pages","parity","preview","watch","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:32.925253621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.282086233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":168,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Taking the next feasible high-impact slice from bv robot triage. I’m auditing preview/watch/pages behavior in src/export_pages.rs and tests/export_pages.rs for another concrete operator-visible failure-path or path-semantics bug.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:09:49Z"},{"id":169,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Found and fixed another preview/pages parity gap in src/export_pages.rs. Problem: the local preview server returned 404 for directory routes without a trailing slash (for example /docs) even when docs/index.html existed, which is weaker than normal static-host behavior and can strand operators when testing nested exported routes. Fix: resolve_preview_asset_path() now serves directory index files when the canonicalized target is a directory containing index.html. Added regression coverage in tests/export_pages.rs: preview_pages_serves_directory_index_without_trailing_slash. Verification via rch: cargo test --test export_pages preview_pages_serves_directory_index_without_trailing_slash -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check was not rerun in this slice; repo still has unrelated pre-existing formatting drift elsewhere.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:11:21Z"},{"id":173,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continuing bd-2bpl.4.2 for another concrete pages/preview/watch slice since the higher-ranked TUI and robot lanes remain active elsewhere. Next pass is focused on preview/watch/pages-wizard behavior in src/export_pages.rs, src/pages_wizard.rs, and focused pages tests.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:20:27Z"},{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verified another concrete pages-wizard parity fix in src/pages_wizard.rs. Problem: when a saved config was missing output_path, the wizard’s repair path sent the operator to Target Settings instead of Export Options, which is the wrong recovery step and can strand the user in the wrong prompt flow. Fix: repair_step_for_saved_config() now returns ExportOptions when output_path is missing before considering deploy_target. Updated the interactive regression wizard_interactive_saved_local_config_missing_output_reprompts_export_options to drive the repaired Export Options -> Deploy Target -> Local Target Config path explicitly. Verification via rch: cargo test --lib pages_wizard::tests::wizard_interactive_saved_local_config_missing_output_reprompts_export_options -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check still fails from unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:26:12Z"},{"id":177,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Found and fixed another concrete preview failure-path mismatch in src/export_pages.rs. Problem: the preview server still served /.bvr/livereload even when --no-live-reload was enabled, which contradicted the advertised disabled status and meant the reload endpoint was not actually disabled. Fix: handle_preview_request() now returns 404 for the reload endpoint when live_reload is false. Added regression coverage in tests/export_pages.rs: preview_pages_no_live_reload_disables_reload_endpoint. Verification via rch: cargo test --test export_pages preview_pages_no_live_reload_disables_reload_endpoint -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check still fails from unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in many other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:29:06Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"CobaltSpring","text":"Audit slice update (CobaltSpring): found and fixed a concrete pages-wizard failure-path bug in src/pages_wizard.rs. Problem: malformed saved configs with an empty output_path ("") were treated as valid because validation only checked whether output_path was None, so the repair flow could skip Export Options instead of prompting the operator to choose a real bundle path. Fix: added WizardConfig::has_output_path() and routed both validate_for_export() and repair_step_for_saved_config() through it so empty PathBuf values are treated as missing output. Added focused regressions for direct validation and the interactive saved-config repair path. Verification via rch: cargo check --all-targets passed; cargo clippy --all-targets passed with only the repo's existing warning backlog; cargo fmt --check remains red repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice. Note: there is unrelated worktree drift in src/tui.rs that I did not touch.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:14Z"},{"id":216,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.2","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"## Preview/Watch/Pages Wizard Audit — FuchsiaAnchor (2026-03-19)

### Methodology
Code-level audit of src/export_pages.rs (export + preview + watch), src/pages_wizard.rs (wizard), src/viewer_assets.rs (embedded assets), and tests/export_pages.rs. Focused on failure-path operator experience.

Note: Prior slices by other agents (2026-03-16/17) already fixed: directory index serving (404 on /docs without slash), --no-live-reload endpoint leaking, wizard repair routing for missing output_path, and empty output_path validation. This audit documents remaining gaps.

### WHAT WORKS WELL
- Self-contained bundle with 24 embedded viewer assets (d3, alpine, WASM graph, sql.js, fonts)
- Preview server with path traversal prevention, symlink escape detection, live reload injection
- 9-step wizard with back-navigation, saved config, repair routing, debug transcript
- Watch mode with debouncing (500ms), error-tolerant re-export, signal handling
- Comprehensive test coverage: bundle structure, issue filtering, title fallback, MIME types, live reload injection, path traversal, empty datasets
- Port hunting (9000-9031) with BVR_PREVIEW_PORT override
- Deploy instructions with shell-quoted arguments for GitHub Pages and Cloudflare

### CRITICAL FAILURE-PATH GAPS

**P1. No pre-flight validation before export.** No check if output_dir exists, is writable, or has disk space. Error surfaces mid-export leaving partial bundle. Operators get generic "io error" with no context about which step failed or how to recover. (src/export_pages.rs:224-300)

**P2. No atomic export.** Writes files sequentially. If export fails partway (disk full, permission denied), bundle is left in inconsistent state. Browser may load half-written JSON during watch/preview. No temp-dir-then-rename pattern.

**P3. Preview server has no read/write timeout.** TcpStream::read() is blocking. Client hang blocks request handler for OS timeout duration (minutes). No timeout set on connections. (src/export_pages.rs:451-547)

**P4. Watch mode silently skips inaccessible files.** File stat failures logged as warnings but `continue`d. Operator may think watch is working when it's not detecting changes on moved/deleted source files. (src/main.rs watch loop)

**P5. Wizard export failure aborts entirely.** If export step fails, wizard prints error and exits. No resume capability. Operator must restart wizard and re-enter all config. (src/pages_wizard.rs:916-923)

### SIGNIFICANT GAPS

**P6. Saved wizard config corruption silent.** Parse failures mapped to InvalidArgument, caller treats as None and starts fresh. Operator loses saved config without warning. (src/pages_wizard.rs:318-320, 362)

**P7. Live reload token computation expensive.** latest_modified_token() does recursive directory traversal on every /.bvr/livereload request. No caching. Performance risk for large bundles with frequent polling (1.2s interval). (src/export_pages.rs:617-636)

**P8. Status endpoint traverses full bundle.** preview_status() recursively counts files on every request. Multiple concurrent requests could cause slowdowns. (src/export_pages.rs:482-492)

**P9. Missing startup context.** If preview bundle path doesn't exist or index.html is missing, error message is generic. No guidance on "run --export-pages first." (src/export_pages.rs:355-366)

**P10. Wizard doesn't check tool availability early.** Users enter DeployTarget → TargetConfig before discovering gh/wrangler missing at Prerequisites step. Wastes operator effort. (src/pages_wizard.rs:843-886)

### MINOR GAPS

**P11. No export transcript in bundle.** Failed watch re-exports only logged to stderr. No persistent record in bundle of export success/failure history.

**P12. Watch debounce allows rapid re-exports.** Debounce prevents detection during window but doesn't prevent rapid re-exports if files keep changing. Could hammer disk.

**P13. Wizard Prerequisites doesn't link to tool installation docs.** Shows "missing: gh" but doesn't say where to install it.

**P14. Partial asset write on failure.** write_viewer_assets() writes sequentially with no rollback. If asset N fails, assets 1..N-1 already on disk.

**P15. No bundle compression or asset versioning.** Assets served with no-cache headers for preview, but no hash-based filenames for production static hosting cache busting.

### TEST COVERAGE GAPS

Already covered: file presence, filtering, title fallback, MIME, live reload, path traversal, empty datasets, method validation.

Not covered:
- Disk full / permission denied scenarios
- Preview server connection timeout behavior
- Partial export recovery
- Watch mode file stat error accumulation
- Wizard config corruption recovery
- Tool availability mocking
- Concurrent watch updates (race conditions)
- Large bundle performance (status endpoint latency)

### RECOMMENDATIONS FOR bd-2bpl.4.4.2

Priority order:
1. P2 (atomic export) + P1 (pre-flight validation) — most impactful, prevents inconsistent bundles
2. P3 (preview read timeout) — prevents hung connections blocking server
3. P5 (wizard export resume) — prevents config re-entry on failure
4. P4 (watch stat error recovery) — prevents silent monitoring failures
5. P7 (live reload token caching) — performance fix for large bundles
","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:16:11Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","title":"Re-audit workspace, hook, and repo-path semantics across all integration surfaces","description":"Purpose:
Many recently fixed bugs came from path-resolution assumptions. This audit must look for the same class of issue across exports, hooks, feedback/config files, workspace loading, watch paths, preview serving, and related operational flows.

Done means:
Path semantics are treated as a first-class parity surface with explicit evidence and follow-up tasks.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:01:36.162604513Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hooks","parity","paths","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:33.596122596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:46.639260766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":145,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"BrightSpire","text":"Audited baseline/drift path semantics and fixed a real workspace-root mismatch.

Problem: --baseline-info, --save-baseline, --check-drift, and --robot-drift were still using raw --repo-path (or .) instead of the resolved load target/project directory. When --repo-path pointed inside a repo that should auto-promote to a workspace root via .bv/workspace.yaml, baseline files were read/written under the nested repo instead of the workspace root.

Fixes landed:
- src/main.rs now routes all four baseline/drift entry points through project_dir_for_load_target(&cli)
- workspace_discovery_start_points() now normalizes relative --repo-path values against current_dir before dedup/discovery, preventing false ambiguity between relative and absolute views of the same workspace root

Evidence/tests:
- added unit test: resolve_issue_load_target_discovers_workspace_from_relative_repo_path_without_ambiguity
- added integration test: baseline_commands_use_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace
- verification passed via rch: cargo test --test cli_model_validation baseline_commands_use_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets
- cargo fmt --check still fails repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice; touched files were rustfmt-formatted locally","created_at":"2026-03-15T03:28:39Z"},{"id":161,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Taking a concrete follow-up under the workspace/path audit: --robot-orphans currently reloads git commits from raw --repo-path / . even though the shared history path already resolves repo root via project_dir_for_load_target() + resolve_repo_root(). In workspace auto-discovery mode that can narrow orphan analysis to the nested repo while issue/history loading is workspace-aware. I am patching that mismatch and adding a nested --repo-path workspace regression.","created_at":"2026-03-16T03:38:40Z"},{"id":162,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"WhiteMink","text":"Landed another concrete workspace/path parity fix in src/main.rs: --robot-orphans now reloads git commits via resolve_repo_root(&cli) instead of raw --repo-path / ., so nested --repo-path invocation that auto-discovers a workspace no longer narrows orphan analysis to the nested repo. Added integration coverage in tests/cli_model_validation.rs for nested repo-path workspace discovery plus workspace-root git history. Manual command-level sanity check shows total_commits=2 and candidate_files includes apps/web/src/orphan.js in that scenario. Standard rch cargo test/check attempts were intermittently blocked by worker /tmp ENOSPC during C dependency compilation; cargo clippy --all-targets did complete on another worker with the repo’s existing warning-only output.","created_at":"2026-03-16T03:43:42Z"},{"id":164,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added another workspace/path fix in src/main.rs: relative history/snapshot references now prefer the resolved project/workspace root before caller cwd shadow files. This closes a class of `--diff-since snapshots/...` bugs when invoking from outside the repo with --repo-path pointing into a workspace-discovered project. Added integration coverage in tests/cli_model_validation.rs for a caller-cwd shadow snapshot versus the intended workspace snapshot. Verification: rch cargo clippy --all-targets passed on vmi1264463 with the repo's existing warning-only output; rch cargo test --test cli_model_validation robot_diff_snapshot_prefers_workspace_relative_file_over_caller_cwd_shadow -- --exact and rch cargo check --all-targets are still active behind a worker-local build lock, so those two are not yet confirmed complete.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:01:15Z"},{"id":198,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update (BronzeGate): found and fixed a concrete workspace/path semantics mismatch in the export surfaces. Problem: when nested --repo-path auto-discovered and promoted to a workspace root, persisted-state paths and hook lookup already used the resolved project/workspace root, but relative --export-pages / --export-md targets still wrote under the caller cwd. Fix: src/export_md.rs now resolves relative export targets against the resolved project/workspace root before both hook env construction and the export callback, and src/main.rs pages export callsites now use the resolved export path. Added regressions in tests/export_md.rs and tests/export_pages.rs proving relative export paths under nested --repo-path workspace discovery land in the workspace root, not the caller cwd, and that BV_EXPORT_PATH matches the resolved target. Verification via rch: cargo test --test export_md export_md_relative_export_path_uses_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace -- --exact; cargo test --test export_pages export_pages_relative_export_path_uses_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check still fails repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:53:54Z"},{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"SapphireLeopard","text":"Progress update (SapphireLeopard): fixed a concrete workspace/path mismatch in the preview surfaces. Problem: relative --preview-pages bundle paths were still resolved from caller cwd, and the interactive --pages wizard inherited the same issue when previewing a saved relative output_path, even when nested --repo-path auto-discovered a workspace root. Fix: src/main.rs now resolves preview bundle paths against the resolved project/workspace root before starting the preview server, and the wizard preview callback uses the same resolver. Added unit coverage in src/main.rs and integration coverage in tests/export_pages.rs. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with existing repo warning backlog only; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo fmt --check passed. Targeted unit test resolve_cli_path_from_project_dir_uses_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace passed; direct end-to-end runtime reproduction confirmed target/debug/bvr --preview-pages bundle --repo-path <nested repo> serves the workspace-root bundle when invoked from outside the workspace.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:01:36Z"}]}

This closes a class of `--diff-since snapshots/...` bugs when invoking from outside the repo with --repo-path pointing into a workspace-discovered project. Added integration coverage in tests/cli_model_validation.rs for a caller-cwd shadow snapshot versus the intended workspace snapshot. Verification: rch cargo clippy --all-targets passed on vmi1264463 with the repo's existing warning-only output; rch cargo test --test cli_model_validation robot_diff_snapshot_prefers_workspace_relative_file_over_caller_cwd_shadow -- --exact and rch cargo check --all-targets are still active behind a worker-local build lock, so those two are not yet confirmed complete.","created_at":"2026-03-16T04:01:15Z"},{"id":198,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update (BronzeGate): found and fixed a concrete workspace/path semantics mismatch in the export surfaces. Problem: when nested --repo-path auto-discovered and promoted to a workspace root, persisted-state paths and hook lookup already used the resolved project/workspace root, but relative --export-pages / --export-md targets still wrote under the caller cwd. Fix: src/export_md.rs now resolves relative export targets against the resolved project/workspace root before both hook env construction and the export callback, and src/main.rs pages export callsites now use the resolved export path. Added regressions in tests/export_md.rs and tests/export_pages.rs proving relative export paths under nested --repo-path workspace discovery land in the workspace root, not the caller cwd, and that BV_EXPORT_PATH matches the resolved target. Verification via rch: cargo test --test export_md export_md_relative_export_path_uses_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace -- --exact; cargo test --test export_pages export_pages_relative_export_path_uses_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace -- --exact; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets. cargo fmt --check still fails repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:53:54Z"},{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"SapphireLeopard","text":"Progress update (SapphireLeopard): fixed a concrete workspace/path mismatch in the preview surfaces. Problem: relative --preview-pages bundle paths were still resolved from caller cwd, and the interactive --pages wizard inherited the same issue when previewing a saved relative output_path, even when nested --repo-path auto-discovered a workspace root. Fix: src/main.rs now resolves preview bundle paths against the resolved project/workspace root before starting the preview server, and the wizard preview callback uses the same resolver. Added unit coverage in src/main.rs and integration coverage in tests/export_pages.rs. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with existing repo warning backlog only; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo fmt --check passed. Targeted unit test resolve_cli_path_from_project_dir_uses_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace passed; direct end-to-end runtime reproduction confirmed target/debug/bvr --preview-pages bundle --repo-path <nested repo> serves the workspace-root bundle when invoked from outside the workspace.","created_at":"2026-03-18T06:01:36Z"},{"id":218,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.3","author":"FuchsiaAnchor","text":"## Workspace/Hook/Repo-Path Audit — FuchsiaAnchor (2026-03-19)

### Methodology
Code-level audit of src/loader.rs (workspace discovery, beads file resolution), src/cli.rs (path flags), src/export_md.rs (hooks), src/main.rs (path resolution), and integration surfaces. Builds on prior fix work by BrightSpire, WhiteMink, BronzeGate, SapphireLeopard.

### ALREADY FIXED (by prior agents during this audit lifecycle)
- Baseline/drift paths now use project_dir_for_load_target, not raw --repo-path (BrightSpire)
- --robot-orphans now uses resolve_repo_root for workspace-aware git loading (WhiteMink)
- Relative history/snapshot refs prefer workspace root over caller cwd (ubuntu)
- Relative --export-pages/--export-md paths resolve against workspace root (BronzeGate)
- Relative --preview-pages paths resolve against workspace root (SapphireLeopard)

### REMAINING GAPS

**W1. Symlink/canonicalization fragility in deduplication.** Workspace repo dedup uses canonicalized paths, but canonicalize() can fail on NFS/FUSE/broken symlinks, falling back to normalization that may not catch duplicates. No test coverage for symlink scenarios. (src/loader.rs)

**W2. Git worktree resolution fragile.** Worktree support reads .git file → commondir pointer → falls back to parent .git. Assumes commondir is 2 levels up from worktree root. Could break with unusual worktree layouts or moved main worktrees. (src/loader.rs)

**W3. Hook stderr not captured or displayed.** Pre/post-export hooks run via Command with stderr set to Stdio::piped() but stderr output is not read or logged. On hook failure, operator gets exit-code only, not the error message. (src/export_md.rs hook execution)

**W4. No warning when multiple preferred .jsonl files exist.** If both beads.jsonl and issues.jsonl exist in .beads/, loader silently picks beads.jsonl. Operator may not realize which file is being used. (src/loader.rs discovery chain)

**W5. Ancestor search stops at first .beads/ match.** If .beads/ exists at both project-root and parent-root, the first match wins. No validation that the found .beads/ is the intended one. (src/loader.rs)

**W6. Hook command runs in current_dir, not project_dir.** Hook env sets BV_PROJECT_DIR but the hook process itself may execute with cwd as caller's directory, not project directory. (src/export_md.rs)

**W7. No per-repo breakdown in robot output.** --robot-triage, --robot-history etc. aggregate across workspace repos but don't break down results by source_repo. Operators can't distinguish which repo contributes what. (src/main.rs, src/robot.rs)

**W8. No per-repo export option.** Export always bundles all workspace repos into one artifact. No flag to export individual repos from a workspace. (src/export_pages.rs)

**W9. History assumes single .git root.** Git history loading uses one git root for all commits. In workspace mode with multiple repos, each repo's git history should be loaded independently. (analysis/git_history.rs)

**W10. No test coverage for deep nesting or cross-mount paths.** Workspace tests cover basic 2-repo aggregation and nested --repo-path promotion, but no tests for deeply nested repos, symlinked repos, or cross-filesystem paths.

### STRONG POINTS
- Discovery chain (--beads-file > --workspace > auto > --repo-path) is well-documented and enforced
- Cross-repo dependency prefixing works correctly (repo:id format)
- Workspace validation catches duplicates, prefix collisions, empty repos
- Recent fixes have materially improved path resolution correctness across baseline/drift, orphans, export, and preview surfaces
- Integration test coverage for workspace discovery scenarios is solid

### RECOMMENDATIONS FOR bd-2bpl.4.4.3

Priority order:
1. W3 (hook stderr capture) — most operator-visible, easy fix
2. W6 (hook cwd) — correctness issue, may cause hook failures silently
3. W4 (multiple .jsonl warning) — prevents confusion
4. W9 (per-repo git history) — workspace correctness
5. W1 (symlink dedup) — edge case but can cause duplicate loading

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","title":"Add export/pages/workspace unit and integration regressions for reopened gaps","description":"Purpose:
